plan.title: Bring task check mypy scope to parity with CI (cover tests/)

narratives.Description: Local task check runs mypy only over run.py, while CI runs mypy over tests/, so a type error under tests/ ships green locally and reddens master after merge. This story expands the local type-check gate to match the CI target set and makes the broader scope fail task check rather than advise.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
Update the core:lint task in the tasks/core.yml file so the mypy invocation covers the same target set as CI, including the tests/ tree, sharing configuration through the pyproject.toml mypy module settings.
Add a test under tests/cli asserting a deliberately introduced type error under tests/ causes the local lint gate to fail with a non-zero exit.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer, I want task check to run mypy with the same scope as CI, so that a type error under tests/ fails locally instead of breaking master.

items.title: Given a deliberately introduced type error under tests/, when task check runs locally, then the type-check gate fails with a non-zero exit.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a deliberately introduced type error under tests/, when task check runs locally, then the type-check gate fails with a non-zero exit.

items.title: When task check runs on a clean tree, then mypy validates the same target set as the CI Python job including the tests/ directory.

narrative.Acceptance: When task check runs on a clean tree, then mypy validates the same target set as the CI Python job including the tests/ directory.

items.title: A regression test validates that the broadened mypy scope fails rather than advising when tests/ contains a type error.

narrative.Acceptance: A regression test validates that the broadened mypy scope fails rather than advising when tests/ contains a type error.

expected_outputs: core:lint mypy scope matches CI including tests/
shared mypy config between local and CI
regression test failing on a tests/ type error

references.title: Issue #1475: task check: type-check parity with CI (run mypy over tests/, not just run.py)
